name: zapier
description: The Zapier CLI is a developer-focused way to create and maintain Zapier integrations. See more at https://github.com/zapier/zapier-platform

subcommands.name: analytics
subcommands.description: Show the status of the analytics that are collected. Also used to change what is collected

options.description: Choose how much information to share. Anonymous mode drops the OS type and Zapier user id, but keeps command info. Identifying information is used only for debugging purposes

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build a pushable zip from the current directory

options.description: Disable "smart" file inclusion. By default, Zapier only includes files that are required by `index.js`. If you (or your dependencies) require files dynamically (such as with `require(someVar)`), then

subcommands.name: convert
subcommands.description: Convert a Legacy Web Builder app or Visual Builder integration to a CLI integration

options.description: Convert a specific version. Required when converting a Visual Builder integration

args.name: integrationId
args.description: To get the integration/app ID, go to "https://zapier.com/app/developer", click on an integration, and copy the number directly after "/app/" in the URL

args.name: path
args.description: Relative to your current path - IE: `.` for current directory

subcommands.name: delete:integration
subcommands.description: Delete your integration (including all versions)

subcommands.name: delete:version
subcommands.description: Delete a specific version of your integration

args.name: version
args.description: Specify the version to delete. It must have no users or Zaps

subcommands.name: deprecate
subcommands.description: Mark a non-production version of your integration as deprecated, with removal by a certain date

args.name: version
args.description: The version to deprecate

args.name: date
args.description: The date (YYYY-MM-DD) when Zapier will make the specified version unavailable

subcommands.name: describe
subcommands.description: Describe the current integraiton

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: env:get
subcommands.description: Get environment variables for a version

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

args.name: version
args.description: The version to get the environment for

subcommands.name: env:set
subcommands.description: Set environment variables for a version

args.name: version
args.description: The version to set the environment for. Values are copied forward when a new version is created, but this command will only ever affect the specified version

args.name: key-value pairs...
args.description: The key-value pairs to set. Keys are case-insensitive. Each pair should be space separated and pairs should be separated by an `=`. For example: `A=123 B=456`

subcommands.name: env:unset
subcommands.description: Unset environment variables for a version

args.name: version
args.description: The version to set the environment for

args.name: keys...
args.description: The keys to unset. Keys are case-insensitive

subcommands.name: history
subcommands.description: Get the history of your integration

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a new Zapier integration with a project template

options.description: The template to start your integration with

args.name: path
args.description: Where to create the new integration. If the directory doesn't exist, it will be created. If the directory isn't empty, we'll ask for confirmation

subcommands.name: integrations
subcommands.description: List integrations you have admin access to

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Link the current directory with an existing integration

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Configure your `~/.zapierrc` with a deploy key

options.description: Use this flag if you log into Zapier a Single Sign-On (SSO) button and don't have a Zapier password

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Deactivate your active deploy key and reset `~/.zapierrc`

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Print recent logs

options.description: Filter logs to the specified version
Filter logs to only see errors or successes
See logs of the specified type
See extra info, like request/response body and headers
Only show logs for this user. Defaults to your account
Cap the number of logs returned. Max is 50 (also the default)
Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Migrate users from one version of your integration to another

args.name: fromVersion
args.description: The version FROM which to migrate users

args.name: toVersion
args.description: The version TO which to migrate users

args.name: percent
args.description: Percentage (between 1 and 100) of users to migrate

subcommands.name: promote
subcommands.description: Promote a specific version to public access

args.name: version
args.description: The version you want promote

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Build and upload the current integration

options.description: Disable "smart" file inclusion. By default, Zapier only includes files that are required by `index.js`. If you (or your dependencies) require files dynamically (such as with `require(someVar)`), then

subcommands.name: register
subcommands.description: Register a new integration in your account

args.name: title
args.description: Your integrations's public title. Asked interactively if not present

subcommands.name: scaffold
subcommands.description: Add a starting trigger, create, search, or resource to your integration

options.description: Specify the new file's directory. Use this flag when you want to create a different folder structure such as `src/triggers` instead of the default `triggers`. Defaults to `[triggers|searches|creates]/

Specify the new test file's directory. Use this flag when you want to create a different folder structure such as `src/triggers` instead of the default `triggers`. Defaults to `test/[triggers|searches
Supply the path to your integration's root (`index.js`). Only needed if your `index.js` is in a subfolder, like `src`
Should we overwrite an existing trigger/search/create file?
When scaffolding, should we skip adding helpful intro comments? Useful if this isn't your first rodeo

subcommands.name: team:add
subcommands.description: Add a team member to your integration

args.name: email
args.description: The user to be invited. If they don't have a Zapier account, they'll be prompted to create one

args.name: role
args.description: The level the invited team member should be at. Admins can edit everything and get email updates. Subscribers only get email updates

args.name: message
args.description: A message sent in the email to your team member, if you need to provide context. Wrap the message in quotes to ensure spaces get saved

subcommands.name: team:get
subcommands.description: Get team members involved with your integration

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: team:remove
subcommands.description: Remove a team member from all versions of your integration

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Test your integration via the "test" script in your "package.json"

options.description: Forgo running `zapier validate` before tests are run. This will speed up tests if you're modifying functionality of an existing integration rather than adding new actions
Use `yarn` instead of `npm`. This happens automatically if there's a `yarn.lock` file, but you can manually force `yarn` if you run tests from a sub-directory

subcommands.name: upload
subcommands.description: Upload the latest build of your integration to Zapier

subcommands.name: users:add
subcommands.description: Add a user to some or all versions of your integration

options.description: Skip confirmation. Useful for running programmatically

args.name: email
args.description: The user to be invited. If they don't have a Zapier account, they'll be prompted to create one

args.name: version
args.description: A version string (like 1.2.3). Optional, used only if you want to invite a user to a specific version instead of all versions

subcommands.name: users:get
subcommands.description: Get a list of users who have been invited to your integration

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: users:links
subcommands.description: Get a list of links that are used to invite users to your integration

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: users:remove
subcommands.description: Remove a user from all versions of your integration

options.description: Skips confirmation. Useful for running programmatically

args.name: email
args.description: The user to be removed

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Validate your integration

options.description: Forgo pinging the Zapier server to run further checks
Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: versions
subcommands.description: List the versions of your integration available for use in the Zapier editor

options.description: Change the way structured data is presented. If "json" or "raw", you can pipe the output of the command into other tools, such as jq

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Display help for <%= config.bin %>

args.name: command
args.description: Command to show help for
